description: M3 Integrations — GitHub clone/merge-queue, localhost runner, Slack notifications, MCP connection manager, WSL support

userStories.title: Create GitHub integration service for cloning repos
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to clone GitHub repos to create projects from existing repositories.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/integrations/github.ts with GitHubService class
cloneRepo(repoUrl, targetPath) clones a GitHub repo using child_process spawn of git
Supports both HTTPS and SSH URLs
Returns { success: boolean, path: string, error?: string }
Validates URL format before cloning
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add GitHub clone endpoint to projects API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a project by providing a GitHub repo URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /api/projects/clone endpoint to server/routes/projects.ts
Accepts { repoUrl: string, name?: string } body
Clones the repo to a workspace directory (data/projects/{name})
Creates a project record in the database with the cloned path
Returns the created project
Input validation: repoUrl must be a valid GitHub URL
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create localhost runner service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to detect and start dev servers for projects automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/integrations/localhost-runner.ts with LocalhostRunner class
detectDevServer(projectPath) checks package.json for dev/start scripts and common frameworks (Next.js, Vite, CRA, Astro)
start(projectPath) spawns the dev server as a child process
stop() kills the running dev server process
getStatus() returns { running: boolean, port: number | null, pid: number | null }
Auto-detects available port if default is occupied
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create localhost runner REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start and stop dev servers from the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/localhost.ts as a Fastify plugin
POST /api/projects/:id/dev-server/start starts the dev server for a project
POST /api/projects/:id/dev-server/stop stops the dev server
GET /api/projects/:id/dev-server/status returns dev server status
Streams dev server logs via WebSocket events ('devserver:log')
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect Project Health tab to localhost runner
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Project Health bottom panel tab to show real dev server status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/localhost.ts with useDevServerStatus(projectId) and useStartDevServer(), useStopDevServer() hooks
Update BottomPanel Project Health tab to show real dev server status
Show start/stop button based on current status
Stream dev server logs via WebSocket subscription
Replace hardcoded health data with real API data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create Slack notification service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service to send Slack messages for pipeline events.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/integrations/slack.ts with SlackNotifier class
sendMessage(webhookUrl, message) sends a POST to a Slack incoming webhook
Supports formatted messages with blocks (title, body, status color)
Helper methods: notifyPipelineComplete, notifyDecisionNeeded, notifyError
Returns { success: boolean, error?: string }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses Slack incoming webhooks — no OAuth needed for v1.

userStories.title: Add Slack webhook configuration to settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure a Slack webhook URL in settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Notifications' tab to SettingsView
Tab shows a Slack webhook URL input field
Save button stores the webhook URL in the settings table (key: 'slack_webhook_url')
Test button sends a test message to verify the webhook works
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create MCP connection manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service to manage connections to MCP servers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/integrations/mcp-manager.ts with McpConnectionManager class
connect(config: { name, command, args, env }) spawns an MCP server as child process
disconnect(name) kills the MCP server process
listConnections() returns all active MCP connections with status
getConnection(name) returns a specific connection
Handles process crashes with auto-restart (max 3 retries)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create MCP connection REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage MCP server connections from the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/mcp.ts as a Fastify plugin
GET /api/mcp/connections returns all MCP connections
POST /api/mcp/connections connects a new MCP server
DELETE /api/mcp/connections/:name disconnects an MCP server
GET /api/mcp/connections/:name/status returns connection status
Input validation on POST body (name, command required)
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add WSL detection utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to detect when running inside WSL and adjust behavior accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/utils/wsl.ts with isWSL() detection function
Checks /proc/version for Microsoft or WSL strings
getVSCodeCommand() returns 'code.exe' on WSL, 'code' otherwise
resolveWSLPath(windowsPath) converts Windows paths to WSL paths
Export utilities from server/utils/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add 'Open in VS Code' support
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a project in VS Code from the sidebar context menu.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /api/projects/:id/open-vscode endpoint
Endpoint spawns the appropriate VS Code command with the project path
Uses WSL detection to choose code vs code.exe
Returns { success: boolean } response
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add project context menu to sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to right-click a project for rename, remove, and Open in VS Code options (PRD US-20).
acceptanceCriteria: Add right-click context menu to project items in LeftSidebar
Menu options: Rename, Remove from list, Open in VS Code
Rename enables inline editing of the project name
Remove from list calls DELETE /api/projects/:id (hides from sidebar)
Open in VS Code calls POST /api/projects/:id/open-vscode
Context menu positioned at cursor location
Click outside dismisses the menu
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add 'Create project from GitHub' dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a project by entering a GitHub URL in the sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Add click handler to the '+' button in the projects sidebar header
Opens a dialog/modal with two options: 'From GitHub repo' and 'Empty project'
From GitHub repo: shows input for repo URL, optional project name
Submit calls POST /api/projects/clone
Empty project: shows input for project name only
Submit calls POST /api/projects
Dialog closes and project list refreshes on success
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
